title: "Privacy Law Roundup — June 07, 2026"
date: 2026-06-07 12:17:24 +0530
categories: [news, roundup]
tags: [dpdpa, india, privacy, global, news]
---

## India: DPDPA Legal Landscape

The Supreme Court is poised to hear multiple petitions challenging the constitutionality of India's Digital Personal Data Protection Act (DPDPA), 2023, and the recently notified Digital Personal Data Protection Rules, 2025. The Court has issued notices on these challenges, signaling a critical phase for the new privacy framework. Legal experts continue to debate the DPDPA's foundational aspects, including concerns over the absence of a statutory definition for "harm," which could lead to significant interpretational uncertainty in future privacy adjudications.
([Source](https://news.google.))

## India: DPDPA Implementation and Readiness

With the DPDPA clock ticking, Indian enterprises are under increasing pressure to achieve compliance, driving a surge in readiness assessments and new technology solutions. UT Chandigarh recently hosted a workshop to raise awareness and guide local entities on DPDPA adherence, highlighting ongoing efforts across various levels of government. Several companies, including SaralPrivacy, GoTrust, and Netskope, have launched new platforms and services, such as automated consent management and India-specific management planes, to help businesses secure data at the source and navigate compliance requirements.
